issues.title: Shared PORT_STEP/PORT_SPREAD_FRAC bump ripples into the out-of-scope class/state edge spread
issues.description: FR4 raised PORT_STEP 20->26 and PORT_SPREAD_FRAC 0.7->0.85 in src/geometry/index.ts, which is shared by the flowchart, class, and state renderers. This visibly shifted class/state edge anchors even though plan.md lists 'Sequence/class/state renderers' as out of scope (see the regenerated test/__snapshots__/class-svg.test.ts.snap and state-svg.test.ts.snap, e.g. class relation source anchors 191.25->188.25 / 181.25->175.25, and examples/svg/{class,state}-*). VERIFIED non-breaking: the anchorBound corner clamp (PORT_MARGIN=6, unchanged) still keeps short sides safe, all snapshots + examples were regenerated, and the full suite is green. Round 3: the cosmetic cross-type label-plate inconsistency this note flagged was closed by the REV-002 fix (native class/state now draw the tight plate too), so the remaining ripple is purely the accepted anchor spread.
issues.proposed_solution: NEEDS-USER-DECISION. The ripple is anticipated by the plan (Approach D explicitly bumps the constant 'shared by both renderers') and is non-breaking / arguably an improvement, so no code change is required to ship. Its label-plate tail was folded into REV-002 and is now resolved.
issues.fix_summary: Orchestrator disposition (2026-07-12): KEEP. The ripple is within the accepted plan (Approach D bumps the constant 'shared by both renderers') and is a strict, non-breaking improvement (anchorBound clamp retained, suite green). Verified by eye: the regenerated class-clean-light and state-clean-light PNGs both read cleanly. The deferred cross-type label-plate inconsistency became REV-002 and is now FIXED + verified in round 3, closing this note's only functional tail.
issues.review_note_r4: UNCHANGED by FR9. Round 5 further raised PORT_STEP 26->30 and REPLACED PORT_SPREAD_FRAC with a border-filling cap (borderLen-2*PORT_MARGIN)/(k-1) (UAT-1 hub fan-in). This is the same accepted shared-geometry ripple class, still non-breaking: sidePoint() unconditionally applies clampOffset(offset, anchorBound(...)), and the border-filling cap lands a rect's extreme anchor EXACTLY on cap (=anchorBound), so no anchor floats off a node; non-rect shapes clamp inward as before. FR4 fan test + 378 tests green. Remains wontfix/keep.
issues.review_note_r5: UNCHANGED by the UAT-round gap+label-vs-edge pivot. This round touches no port/anchor geometry (PORT_STEP still 30, cap unchanged); the only new shared constant is LABEL_NODE_PAD 6->10, which affects the label-vs-node push only (not anchors). Same accepted ripple class, still non-breaking; 385 unit green. Remains wontfix/keep.
issues.review_note_r6: UNCHANGED by D12. D12 alters only the port ORDERING KEY (heading vs far-centre), not PORT_STEP/PORT_MARGIN or the spread magnitude, and it is a proven no-op wherever an edge has no detour bend. Adversarially confirmed the class/state ripple is NOT D12: class edges carry 0 detour waypoints across every fixture/example (examples/src/class.mmd, fixtures/shop-class.mmd) so D12 falls back to centre-order byte-for-byte; the class snapshot's +/-10->+/-15 source spread is purely this accepted PORT_STEP accumulation. Remains wontfix/keep.

issues.title: FR6 de-collides class/state labels using the tight plate size, but native class/state SVG draws the OLD looser plate - so 'no two plates overlap' is not actually enforced for 2 of the 3 D5-scoped tiers
issues.description: FR6's guarantee (plan.md: 'no two edge-label plates may overlap') is scoped by D5 to flowchart + state + class. For FLOWCHART it holds: deCollideLabels (src/layout/index.ts) sizes plates via labelPlateSize = 0.6*size+6 / lines*lh+2, and the flowchart sink src/render/svg.ts edgeLabel draws EXACTLY that. But the native class/state static renderers drew their labels with their own LOCAL edgeLabel using the pre-FR3 looser formula: src/native/class/svg.ts and src/native/state/svg.ts both used `w = label.length * font.size * 0.62 + 10` (and `h = lineHeight + 4`). So for class/state, FR6 separated plates assuming the TIGHT size, while the tier actually painted the WIDER loose plate at those same centres -> the drawn plates could STILL OVERLAP. VERIFIED with the shipped resolveLabelCollisions: two colliding tight plates (labels 'commit to durable storage' / 'escalate to operator now', size 14) are cleared to a 6px gap on x, but the loose plates class/state actually drew still overlapped by ~4.86px on x. The FR6 test and the fixture-invariant test both sized plates with labelPlateSize (tight) on BOTH the de-collision AND the assertion, so they passed while the actually-rendered class/state plates could overlap - a false-confidence guard for those tiers.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Unify the native class/state edge-label plate with the FR3/FR6 formula so the drawn plate matches what the de-collision assumes. In src/native/class/svg.ts and src/native/state/svg.ts edgeLabel, replace the local `0.62+10 / +4` calc with labelPlateSize (ideally import/reuse the one function), regenerate the class/state snapshots + examples, add a drawn-plate regression guard that asserts the EMITTED rect (not labelPlateSize on both sides), and re-run the suite.
issues.fix_summary: Unified the native class + state edge-label plate with the shared labelPlateSize (0.6*size+6 / lines*lh+2): src/native/class/svg.ts:158 and src/native/state/svg.ts:108 edgeLabel now call `labelPlateSize(label, theme)!` instead of the old 0.62*size+10 / +4, so the drawn plate matches what FR6 deCollideLabels assumed -> de-collided class/state labels no longer overlap. Added drawn-plate regression guards (class-svg + state-svg tests) that extract the REAL emitted `<rect>` adjacent to the `<text>` and assert width/height == labelPlateSize; switched state-svg's TEST-006 helper to labelPlateSize. Regenerated class/state snapshots + examples.
issues.review_note_r3: VERIFIED RESOLVED (round 3). (1) Both native edgeLabel functions now call labelPlateSize(label, theme)! - the exact size deCollideLabels/plateSizeOf use, so the drawn plate == the de-collision plate for all three D5 tiers; the earlier long-label collision that overlapped by ~4.86px now clears (drawn plate == tight plate == de-collided plate). (2) The svg.ts diff is minimal and in-scope (import + formula swap only, nothing else touched). (3) The regression guards genuinely assert the EMITTED rect vs labelPlateSize (regex on the SVG string, not labelPlateSize on both sides) - a real drift guard; a revert to 0.62*size+10 would fail them; both PASS. (4) Snapshot delta is exactly the plate shrink (extends: w 70.76->64.8=7*14*0.6+6, h 22->20=18+2); the anchor shifts present are the already-accepted round-1 REV-001 ripple, not new; viewBox/cards/markers unchanged. (5) Regenerated class-clean-light + state-clean-light PNGs show no label clipping (fetch/2xx/fail/retry legible). (6) Sequence edgeLabel confirmed UNCHANGED (still 0.62*size+10, out of scope). (7) No import cycle: layout/index.ts imports no native/*; build incl. DTS green; CLI child_process tests exercise the path at runtime with no init-order break.
issues.review_note_r4: Still RESOLVED and unaffected by FR9. The FR9 round only added the shared finishEdges label passes upstream of the same labelPlateSize; both native edgeLabel sinks still call labelPlateSize (verified via git diff master src/native/class/svg.ts + state/svg.ts - only the labelPlateSize swap, no revert). class-svg/state-svg drawn-plate guards remain green in the 378-test run.
issues.review_note_r5: Still RESOLVED. The new label-vs-edge de-collision (deCollideLabelsFromEdges) sizes its plates with the SAME shared labelPlateSize (src/layout/index.ts:85), so it de-collides against the drawn size for all three tiers - no reopening of the drift class. git diff master src/native/{class,state}/svg.ts still shows only the labelPlateSize swap. class-svg/state-svg drawn-plate guards green in the 385-test run.
issues.review_note_r6: Still RESOLVED. D12 touches port ORDERING only; it does not change any label-plate sizing sink. git diff still shows src/native/{class,state}/svg.ts edgeLabel calling the shared labelPlateSize (no revert). class-svg/state-svg drawn-plate guards green in the 387-test run.

issues.title: renderSvgAsync drops opts.bridges for class/state, so `bridges:false` is silently ignored on that public API path
issues.description: D4 specifies the bridges toggle forces on/off across the API (SvgRenderOptions.bridges). It is correctly threaded through the sync renderSvg (flowchart), the CLI, the runtime payload, and renderHtmlAsync. BUT the public async library entry src/render/route.ts renderSvgAsync built the STATIC class/state SVG via `renderClassSvg(layoutClass(model, { theme }), ...)` / `renderStateSvg(layoutState(model, { theme }), ...)` - neither forwarded opts.bridges. Because the per-style default is `bridges ?? true` (ON for elbow), a caller doing `renderSvgAsync(classOrStateDsl, { bridges: false })` still got bridges baked into edge.path. The opt-OUT was dropped; the safe default (ON) was preserved.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Thread the option in src/render/route.ts renderSvgAsync: pass `{ theme, bridges: opts.bridges }` into the class and state layout calls (as the CLI's doClassRender/doStateRender already do). One-line each; no snapshot churn for the default case (undefined still resolves to the same ON default).
issues.fix_summary: src/render/route.ts renderSvgAsync now passes { theme, bridges: opts.bridges } into layoutClass (line 67) and layoutState (line 69); renderHtmlAsync threads it too (lines 89, 91) for consistency. Default (undefined) still resolves to the same ON-for-elbow behavior, so no snapshot churn.
issues.review_note_r3: VERIFIED RESOLVED (round 3). Confirmed in src/render/route.ts: renderSvgAsync class case (line 67) and state case (line 69) both forward `bridges: opts.bridges`; renderHtmlAsync class/state (lines 89, 91) too. No remaining class/state layout call on the async surface drops the option (sequence is correctly excluded; flowchart delegates to the sync renderSvg which already threads it). `renderSvgAsync(classOrStateDsl, { bridges: false })` now honors the opt-out; undefined still resolves ON-for-elbow via the `?? true` gate (no snapshot churn).
issues.review_note_r4: Still RESOLVED. git diff master confirms route.ts renderSvgAsync/renderHtmlAsync forward bridges into layoutClass/layoutState (unchanged from round 3). FR9 rides the same finishEdges gate; no new async call site drops the option.
issues.review_note_r5: Still RESOLVED. The FR7 arc->gap pivot did not change the toggle surface: applyBridges' gate is unchanged (`theme.edgeStyle === 'elbow' && (bridges ?? true)`, src/layout/index.ts:43) and the runtime twin's bridgesEnabled matches. The gap render-svg test 'bridges: false removes every gap' and e2e '--no-bridges strips every gap' both pass; route.ts still threads bridges into class/state. No new async call site drops the option.
issues.review_note_r6: Still RESOLVED. D12 added a new geometry PARAM (`bends`) to computePerimeterPorts but no new render option; it introduces no new layoutClass/layoutState call site. route.ts renderSvgAsync/renderHtmlAsync still thread bridges into class/state. Unaffected.

issues.title: Diagram bounds/viewBox exclude edge-label plates, so an outward FR6 nudge could clip a label at the viewBox edge (not observed in the corpus)
issues.description: contentBounds (src/geometry/index.ts) computes the viewBox from node boxes + raw edge POINTS only - it never considers edge-label plate extents. FR6 can move a label's centre off its edge midpoint outward, and the label plate extends +/- half-plate beyond the centre, so a label near the diagram perimeter could extend past the viewBox (which resvg hard-clips in PNG export). This is largely pre-existing (label plates were never in the bounds), but FR6's outward nudge marginally raises the risk. VERIFIED it does NOT currently materialise: scanning every fixture plus the TEST-001 repro shape, no de-collided label plate extends beyond its computed viewBox (BOUNDS_PADDING=20 absorbs it in practice). Reported as a latent risk, not a demonstrated defect.
issues.proposed_solution: NEEDS-USER-DECISION (low priority). No change needed to ship (no clip observed). If a robust guarantee is wanted, fold each labelled edge's plate rect (labelPos +/- labelPlateSize/2) into the extraPoints passed to contentBounds in layout()/applyPositions() so the viewBox always encloses labels; mirror the same in the runtime's boundsAbs for parity. A cheap alternative is a fixture-invariant assertion that every label plate lies within the reported bounds.
issues.fix_summary: Orchestrator disposition (2026-07-12): KEEP as-is for this pass. Pre-existing (label plates were never in contentBounds) and VERIFIED not to materialise across every fixture + the repro (BOUNDS_PADDING=20 absorbs the FR6 nudge). Folding label-plate extents into contentBounds/boundsAbs would re-churn EVERY diagram's viewBox (all snapshots + examples) for a non-observed clip, and must be parity-mirrored - net-negative trade for this pass.
issues.review_note_r3: DISPOSITION CONFIRMED REASONABLE (round 3). The REV-002 fix TIGHTENED the class/state plate (64.8 vs 70.76 wide, 20 vs 22 tall), so if anything it slightly REDUCES the perimeter-clip surface for those tiers, not increases it. The clip remains unobserved across the corpus and both regenerated class/state PNGs show no edge-clipping. Deferring the contentBounds/boundsAbs enclosure (which would churn every viewBox and needs a parity mirror) for a non-observed clip is a sound trade for this pass. Latent nit for a future scoped pass; no code expected this round.
issues.review_note_r4: Still WONTFIX, and FR9 slightly touches this surface: separateLanes runs BEFORE contentBounds in layout() (line 266 finishEdges, then line 268 allEdgePoints = edges.flatMap points), so the re-laned POINTS are enclosed by the viewBox (verified in code) - the moved runs are NOT clipped. The label-vs-node pass can push a plate outward (like FR6), same latent (still-unobserved) plate-vs-viewBox risk this nit already covers; no new clip observed across fixtures + repro. No code expected.
issues.review_note_r5: Still WONTFIX. The new label-vs-edge slide (resolveLabelEdgeCollisions) is a THIRD label-moving pass that can push a plate centre outward (along its own run), sharing the same latent plate-vs-viewBox surface. Still unobserved: the fresh-build repro PNG + regenerated flowchart/class/state examples show no perimeter clip. See REV-008 for the related (also-latent) 'no clamp to the own-run extent' behavior of that same pass. No code expected.
issues.review_note_r6: Still WONTFIX and unaffected by D12. D12 changes port ORDERING only; it does not move any label plate and does not change contentBounds. The edge POINTS a reordered edge produces are still enclosed by contentBounds (unchanged). No new perimeter clip in the fresh-build repro PNG or the regenerated examples. No code expected.

issues.title: Two crossings within 2*BRIDGE_RADIUS on the same edge segment splice a backward-L hop sequence (valid SVG, minor visual overlap) - not observed in the corpus
issues.description: bridgedPath (src/geometry/index.ts + runtime twin) sorts a segment's hops by distance-from-start and emits `... Q <at1+r> L <at2-r> Q ...`. The corner-guard only skipped crossings within BRIDGE_RADIUS (5) of a SEGMENT END, not two crossings near each other. If two distinct crossings land on the same segment less than 2*BRIDGE_RADIUS (10 units) apart, the entry point of the second hop (at2 - 5) precedes the exit of the first (at1 + 5), so the `L` doubles back along the segment. The output was still syntactically VALID SVG and byte-parity-safe - only a small visual artifact of overlapping bumps. VERIFIED it did NOT occur across the current fixtures/snapshots; reported for completeness.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (optional, low priority). When a segment carries multiple hops, drop any hop whose distance-from-start is within 2*BRIDGE_RADIUS of a previously placed hop on that segment (deterministic 'keep the earlier'), applied identically in geometry.bridgedPath and the runtime twin to preserve byte-parity.
issues.fix_summary: Hardened bridgedPath in geometry + the runtime twin: when a segment carries multiple hops (sorted by distance-from-start), skip any hop within 2*BRIDGE_RADIUS (10) of the last-placed hop on that segment ('keep the earlier'), preventing the backward-L overlap. Deterministic + applied identically in both twins (byte-parity preserved). Added a geometry unit test (two crossings 5px apart on one segment -> exactly one hop).
issues.review_note_r3: VERIFIED RESOLVED (round 3). geometry.bridgedPath (src/geometry/index.ts:550-563) and the runtime twin (src/render/dom/runtime.ts:1210-1222) are byte-identical in the skip logic: both init `lastHopDist = -Infinity`, both skip when `h.dist - lastHopDist < 2*BRIDGE_RADIUS` (geometry) / `< 10` (runtime, 2*5=10), both update lastHopDist only on a placed hop, both emit `L ...-5 Q ...*2 ...+5`, both sort segHops by `p.dist - q.dist`, and both applyEdgeBridges push `dist: dEntry = hypot(x - s1)` identically - so a multi-hop segment can't break byte-parity. New geometry test 'collapses two crossings closer than 2*radius on one segment to a single hop (REV-005)' PASSES; dom-runtime-parity (32 tests) green.
issues.review_note_r4: Still RESOLVED. Re-confirmed the geometry.bridgedPath skip logic and its runtime twin are byte-identical in the current diff (both `< 2*BRIDGE_RADIUS`/`< 10`, sort by dist, emit -5 Q *2 +5). FR9 runs BEFORE bridges and can introduce new crossings the lane jogs create, but those flow through the same guarded bridgedPath; the REV-005 geometry test still PASSES in the 378-test run.
issues.review_note_r5: Still RESOLVED after the arc->GAP pivot, which carried the guard over intact. bridgedPath is renamed gappedPath (BRIDGE_RADIUS 5 -> GAP_RADIUS 4); the same-segment overlap skip is now `g.dist - lastGapDist < 2*GAP_RADIUS` (geometry, =8) and `< 8` (runtime literal) - byte-identical in both twins, same sort-by-dist, same `lastGapDist=-Infinity` init, emitting `L ..-4 M ..+4` instead of the old `Q`. The REV-005 test was flipped to gaps: 'collapses two crossings closer than 2*radius on the under-line to a single gap (REV-005)' (test/geometry.test.ts:381) PASSES; the twin is exercised by the dom-runtime-parity crossing-gap fixture. Verified byte-identical.
issues.review_note_r6: Still RESOLVED, unaffected by D12. gappedPath / the same-segment overlap skip are untouched by the port-ordering change. D12 can change WHICH port an edge leaves (hence where crossings fall), but every crossing still flows through the same guarded gappedPath in both twins. REV-005 gap test green in the 387-test run.

issues.title: decisions.md D10 records the REJECTED 'space-at-source' path (separateLanes 'shelved, unwired') - it contradicts the shipped code, which wires the offset separateLanes into finishEdges
issues.description: AUDIT-TRAIL inconsistency (not product code). decisions.md D10 'RESOLVED (user, 2026-07-13) -> B (space at the source)' states the offset approach jogged and the user chose the dagre edgesep/nodesep/ranksep + perimeter 'space at the source', and explicitly that '`separateLanes` (the offset prototype) is shelved (exported, unwired)'. But the shipped code does the OPPOSITE: separateLanes IS exported AND wired as the FIRST step of finishEdges (src/layout/index.ts finishEdges -> separateLanes; mirrored in runtime.ts renderEdges/buildSvg), and there is no dagre edgesep 'space-at-source' change. The reversal is recorded only in state.md ('D10 reversal (2026-07-13): ... reverted to the OFFSET (separateLanes). Offset is the settled FR9 approach.') and matches plan.md FR9/Approach E. So decisions.md D10 was never given a 'reversal' RESOLVED sub-block and now misleads: a reader following D10 verbatim would believe separateLanes is dead code. VERIFIED: read decisions.md D10 (lines 254-285) vs finishEdges + state.md line 35. Confirmed the CODE (source of truth) matches the orchestrator's task brief and plan.md, not the D10 block.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (docs only). Append a 'RESOLVED - reversal (user, 2026-07-13)' sub-block to decisions.md D10 recording that, on seeing the global space-at-source spread read WORSE than the compact offset, the user reverted to the offset separateLanes (now wired into finishEdges), mirroring the state.md D10-reversal line and plan.md FR9. No product-code change; this only repairs the audit trail so a future maintainer doesn't treat wired separateLanes as shelved.
issues.fix_summary: Appended a D10 REVERSAL block to decisions.md: edgesep 'space at the source' (D10=B) was rejected on sight ('6-edgesep30 looks much much worse than 5-fr9'); the compact OFFSET separateLanes ships, wired first in finishEdges + mirrored in the runtime twin. Decisions log now matches the shipped code.
issues.review_note_r5: VERIFIED RESOLVED (round 5). decisions.md:287 now carries the 'REVERSED (user, 2026-07-13): D10=B rejected on sight -> the OFFSET (separateLanes) ships' sub-block, matching the shipped finishEdges (separateLanes wired first) + state.md + plan.md FR9. The audit trail no longer contradicts the code.
issues.review_note_r6: Still RESOLVED. Docs-only; unrelated to D12. The new decisions.md D12 entry (heading-order ports) is a separate, internally-consistent block that matches the shipped computePerimeterPorts `bends` param + its three wired call sites. No new audit-trail contradiction introduced.

issues.title: FR9's lane moves are never exercised by the 'no edge cuts a non-endpoint node' / orthogonality invariants - separateLanes is a no-op on every shipped fixture; the only re-laning shape (inline repro) is checked for byte-parity but not geometry
issues.description: The plan's Tests table calls for the FR9 fixtures to assert 'every edge's polyline stays orthogonal + connected to both anchors (no detached ends)' and (via the existing invariant) no new edge-through-node crossings. VERIFIED GAP: the 'leaves no edge cutting through a non-endpoint node in any shipped fixture' invariant (test/layout.test.ts:138 edgeThroughNodeCount) iterates only fixtures/*.mmd. A probe against a fresh build shows separateLanes fires on NONE of the 9 fixtures - 0 bundles of >=3 near-parallel interior vertical runs within LANE_GAP(26)/LANE_MIN_OVERLAP(40) post-layout (max interior vSegs per fixture = 4, none bundled). So that invariant runs ONLY where separateLanes is a no-op - it never checks a re-laned diagram. The single shape that actually re-lanes is the inline reproDsl: in dom-runtime-parity.test.ts it asserts byte-parity + lanes.size>=3, and in layout.test.ts the label-vs-node test asserts plate-vs-node clearance - but NEITHER asserts the polyline stays orthogonal/connected or that a moved run doesn't cut through a node. Since FR9 shifts interior segments by up to (k-1)/2*LANE_GAP (+/-26 for a 3-bundle), a lane move CAN in principle push a vertical run through a node sitting in that channel; there is currently no automated regression guard for that on any re-laning shape (only byte-parity + eye-verification per state.md). The design (interior-only detection i>=1 && i+2<len so no anchor detaches; simplify() guarantees perpendicular neighbours so the cascade keeps the elbow orthogonal; bounded LANE_GAP move) makes it unlikely, and it is eye-verified, so this is a coverage gap, not a demonstrated defect.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Add an FR9 geometry assertion on a shape that actually re-lanes (the reproDsl): run it through edgeThroughNodeCount (expect 0), and assert every re-laned edge's points still alternate strictly H/V (orthogonal, no diagonal) with points[0]/points[len-1] unchanged from the pre-separateLanes anchors, plus an idempotency check (running separateLanes twice == once). Keep it in test/layout.test.ts or geometry.test.ts so a future separateLanes change that routes a run through a node or breaks the elbow is caught, not just an eye-check.
issues.fix_summary: Added FR9 invariant tests: geometry unit test drives separateLanes on a real 3-run merged bundle and asserts spread >= LANE_GAP, orthogonal + anchors unmoved, deterministic/idempotent, elbow-only (no-op for curved/lone run); layout test lays out the re-laning repro DSL and asserts edgeThroughNodeCount==0 + every edge stays axis-aligned + no 3 near-parallel long runs remain merged. 383 unit green.
issues.review_note_r5: VERIFIED RESOLVED (round 5). The re-laning shape now has real geometry guards: test/layout.test.ts:202 'FR9: lane separation stays orthogonal + cuts no node (a re-laning diagram)' asserts edgeThroughNodeCount(pos)==0 (line 231) AND every routed segment axis-aligned (line 236) on a diagram where separateLanes actually fires; test/geometry.test.ts:271 drives 3 near-parallel interior verticals to >= LANE_GAP keeping paths orthogonal + anchored, and geometry.test.ts:406 checks orthogonality/idempotency. All green in the 385-test run. The gap+label-vs-edge pivot does not touch separateLanes.
issues.review_note_r6: Still RESOLVED. D12 does not touch separateLanes; the re-laning geometry guards remain green in the 387-test run. Note the reproDsl (the re-laning shape those guards use) is now ALSO the shape that exercises D12's port reorder, so the same layout.test.ts fixture doubles as the D12 integration surface (see the new 'orders the API Gateway fan by heading' test at layout.test.ts:276).

issues.title: Label-vs-edge slide has no clamp to its own run's extent (and the edge/node label passes don't jointly converge) - a heuristic that can leave a label off its own line or grazing a parallel neighbour; not observed to fail in the corpus
issues.description: The new resolveLabelEdgeCollisions (src/geometry/index.ts:556; runtime twin src/render/dom/runtime.ts:1419) slides a label along its own edge's nearest-run axis to clear PERPENDICULAR foreign crossing lines. It is deterministic and parity-safe (verified byte-identical twins: same 0.5 orientation thresholds, PORT_LABEL_PAD=6, 4 bounded passes, nearestRunAxis, hiTarget/loTarget accumulation, +axis tiebreak, rounded-centre / only-move-on-overlap writeback). Two heuristic limits are real and code-grounded: (1) the slide target (gx +/- hw + PORT_LABEL_PAD) is NOT clamped to the label's own run interval [lo,hi], so in pathological dense geometry it could slide the plate centre past the end of its own edge segment (label floats off the line it labels) or outward past the viewBox (see REV-004). (2) The label passes run once each in sequence (label-label -> label-vs-edge -> label-vs-node -> bridges) and do NOT re-iterate to a joint fixpoint, so the final label-vs-node push could in principle nudge a label back toward a foreign line the edge pass had cleared. By design it also deliberately leaves PARALLEL grazes alone (an along-axis move can't clear them) - state.md discloses 'batch load still grazes 1 parallel neighbour lane'. VERIFIED none of these materialise as a defect: the gRPC-stream target clears 0 foreign segments AND 0 node overlap simultaneously (test/layout.test.ts:397,366), and the fresh-build repro PNG + regenerated flowchart/class/state examples show every label on its line and inside bounds. Reported as a latent limitation of a new mechanism, not a demonstrated defect.
issues.proposed_solution: NEEDS-USER-DECISION (low priority, no change needed to ship). The pass is an accepted best-effort de-collision and the target case is verified clean. If a hard guarantee is wanted later: (a) clamp the slide target to the own-run interval [lo+hw, hi-hw] (fall back to the smaller residual overlap if the run is too short), mirrored byte-for-byte in the runtime twin; and/or (b) fold the label plate into contentBounds (the REV-004 enclosure) so an outward slide can never clip. The parallel-graze residual is the deferred lane/left-right-port follow-up already noted in D11 - out of scope for this pass.
issues.review_note_r6: OUT OF D12 SCOPE - unchanged. D12 does not touch resolveLabelEdgeCollisions or any label-moving pass; the heuristic limits described here are neither improved nor worsened by the port-ordering change. Still a latent, unobserved nit; remains open pending the deferred left/right-port + own-run-clamp follow-up. Interestingly D12 incidentally moved some state labels off their lines as a side benefit (decisions.md D12) via the reorder, but that is orthogonal to this pass's clamp limitation.
issues.fix_summary: Latent nit (needs-user-decision): the label-vs-edge slide has no self-run clamp and the label passes don't jointly re-converge. Reviewer verified it does NOT materialise across the corpus/repro. Accepted as a DOCUMENTED follow-up at the user's D13 accept & ship (listed in report Follow-ups). Not a ship blocker.

issues.title: D12's state re-route port-heading reorder (native/state/layout.ts) has no dedicated committed byte-parity guard - covered only transitively by the shared runtime computePorts + the flowchart reproDsl parity test
issues.description: D12 adds a THIRD static call site that feeds detour bends into computePerimeterPorts: src/native/state/layout.ts:100 (the pseudo-state shrink re-route). Unlike class (0 detour waypoints on every fixture -> D12 is a proven byte-for-byte no-op there) and the inline state-svg snapshot MODEL (linear, no fan-in -> also a no-op, so the state snapshot is unchanged), the order-state fixture GENUINELY reorders under D12: two transitions entering the end pseudo-state swap their target-side ports (Crashed->root_end offset +5->-5, Idle->root_end -5->+5, across light/dark/fancy). That state re-route path is exercised only by test/cli.test.ts (renders, no golden byte-compare) and the e2e specs (browser interaction, no static-vs-runtime byte-compare); the committed dom-runtime-parity STATE cases (test/dom-runtime-parity.test.ts:722) use a linear 3-node model with no fan-in, so they never drive a state port reorder. VERIFIED CURRENTLY CLEAN (not a defect): rendered order-state static (renderStateSvg) vs the live runtime (mountState via jsdom) -> identical relative edge geometry through the reordered fan-in. The twin LOGIC itself is guarded (computePorts is the single shared function for all diagram types, and the flowchart reproDsl parity test at dom-runtime-parity.test.ts:633 drives D12 reorders on BOTH the source side (4 edges) AND the target side (2 edges into HUB) and byte-compares toSvgString()==renderSvgFromModel). So the risk is a COVERAGE gap on the state WIRING/serialization branch, not a demonstrated drift - low risk because state reuses the flowchart runtime unchanged, but this is exactly the twin-drift class the project's own standards flag ('parity guards must cover EVERY branch of a serialized twin'; 'interactive class/state reuse the flowchart runtime').
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Add a state fan-in case to test/dom-runtime-parity.test.ts that mounts the state runtime (or drives buildPayload(stateLayout.model)) and byte-compares to renderStateSvg for a diagram whose pseudo-state re-route actually reorders ports under D12 (e.g. two transitions into a shared `[*]` end, matching order-state). This closes the one D12 static call site not currently covered by a committed byte-parity guard, so a future change to native/state/layout.ts, the pseudo-shrink, or waypoint serialization cannot silently drift the state runtime from the static SVG. Optional: also add a layout-level assertion that the order-state fan-in is crossing-free after the heading reorder (mirrors the flowchart API-fan guard at layout.test.ts:276).
issues.fix_summary: Added a dedicated state-reroute parity guard to test/dom-runtime-parity.test.ts: baked renderStateSvg edges (native/state/layout.ts ports) vs the live mountState runtime for the order-state fixture (fans into [*] end + out of Running, genuinely reorders under D12), compared by offset-invariant relative geometry. Bite-verified: reverting native/state to center-order fails it.
